metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1441
metadata.comment: Rationale: Fig2C and D: Purkinje cells from betaIII spectrin null mice have a lower spine density in culture. This is verified in intact tissue in Fig3, where betaIII spectrin null mice have a lower spine density in Purkinje neurons while the spines of pyramidal neurons are unaltered. This leads to an overall loss of synapses in the cerebellum as shown in fig4, while synapses in the CA1 of hippocampus are spared. However, betaII spectrin seems to fulfill a similar role in cultured hippocampal neurons (see additional ref PMID 28576936). Experimental description: M&M: "Both WT and beta-III -/- mice were...."
